## [00:00 - 03:02]

This is a pubcast shot. I'm John Loomer Let results be your guide Let's go So if you've been following my stuff long enough You've surely heard me use this phrase and probably a whole bunch of times let results be your guide Let's talk about what that means and how it can help with your advertising approach Now I've found that new advertisers or really any advertisers who lack confidence or conviction in what they're doing Desire structure and certainty they want a repeatable approach that they can apply to all of their Advertising and that can become a problem So let me give you a few examples of the questions that I get to help this make a little bit more sense How often should I update creative right common question? They're looking for a regular cadence so that they can add it to their routine Update the creative every three weeks four weeks two months Whatever if they know that they need to create new ads every three weeks it adds purpose and direction But the reality of course is much messier The need to update creative will differ based on a whole lot of factors So potential audience size right are they targeting internationally? Is it by country and if so which countries because different audience sizes by country or is it local? Because local could change things completely and what is your budget because obviously the higher the budget the more often you may need to cycle through creative and also the number of creative options that you're running at once Because if you put everything into one ad then at that point Yeah, you may need to update creative more often But if you've got six ads running at once then that kind of balances things out a little bit now the need to update Creative is largely driven by creative fatigue and what we want to avoid right is creative fatigue So in theory once someone has seen your ad a certain number of times the likelihood of converting eventually drops At that point any new impression that they get is mostly a waste of money But that threshold is going to be wildly different Depending on the situation because in some cases Lots of frequency is necessary Especially for bigger commitments higher price points But in others you may only need to reach people a handful of times like for a free offer Now regardless there is no universal number of impressions That will trigger the need for new creative and there certainly isn't a cadence of amount of time that should pass

## [03:02 - 06:06]

All that said meta should notify you when creative fatigue has become an issue and really this is far easier than you think Let results be your guide Let's assume you were initially getting great results for several weeks or even months But you've noticed that performance has trended downward with time your ads are no longer effective So how often should you update creative? Let results be your guide Results can fluctuate from day to day or even week to week But if you've seen a consistent trend and you're no longer getting the results you once were it's time to try something else Let's move on to another common question. Should I restrict by age or gender comes up a lot Now I understand the inclination to define your ideal customer by age and gender Maybe you predominantly serve women age 25 to 44 so you define your audience that way, but is it necessary? First these inputs are only used as suggestions when advanced plus audience is on so if it actually is necessary You may need to turn advanced plus audience off first Now in theory this shouldn't be necessary When using the performance goal to maximize conversions when your conversion event is a purchase Meta is focused on getting you more purchases and will make adjustments in your audience to help make that happen Now I've seen examples of this where the business served women Entrepreneurs as an example they kept gender open and the vast majority of their budget was still spent on women And they also received a purchase from someone whose gender was unknown to meta and this person would not have been reached If the audience were restricted you also don't want to completely eliminate a potential customer who may have otherwise bought from you An example would be you may sell men's clothing But that doesn't mean that only men would buy a man's partner spouse or parent may also buy your product as a gift There are times when restricting by age or gender may be necessary Now I've seen this in some cases when optimizing for leads. I was getting lots of cheap leads But most were coming from people over the age of 65. They were also low quality leads I had to turn advanced plus audience off in order to restrict the age limit in this case And that doesn't mean that this is always necessary when optimizing for leads I've looked at plenty of examples of accounts where budget was distributed naturally between age groups Even while using advanced plus audience. So don't assume it So should you restrict by age or gender? You know the answer Let results be your guide. It's not just your cost per conversion It's the quality of your results too because in my example with the leads

## [06:06 - 08:39]

I was getting lots of cheap leads, but they were low quality Run a breakdown of your results by age or gender to see how your budget is distributed and where the results are coming from If you primarily sell to women and 90% of your budget is spent on women There's likely no need to make a change. You want to limit restrictions when possible But if 80% of your budget is spent on men and they aren't buying from you It is a sign that something needs to change. So here's the bottom of the glass This is what I want you to walk away with today There are so many other examples of this right? Should you use lookalike audiences or go completely broad? Should you use single image ads or catalog ads? Should you use advanced plus placements or remove some placements? You know what you should say? Let results be your guide Don't rely on what others are doing. Don't look for universal truths about what works or doesn't work for everyone Find what works for you make changes based on results Your results not based on theories or your gut or anything else Now there is a bit of a caveat here and it's related to making changes based on meaningful results We'll cover that in the next episode Thanks again so much for joining me on the pubcast I really do appreciate it and if you're a regular or you want to become a regular There are a couple things you can do so first up no matter where you are whether it's Apple podcast Spotify On my website somewhere else make sure that you subscribe rate and review this more than anything else Will help me grow and I truly appreciate it second subscribe to podcast alerts when you do I will notify you every time I've published a new episode It will include a brief explanation a brief summary of what was discussed in a link to my website where that podcast and Transcript are embedded to subscribe go to John Loomer comm slash Podcast either way.
